title: Immutable Audit Trail for All Material Changes
description: The system shall maintain an immutable audit trail for all material business and configuration changes across plan management, eligibility, enrollment, carrier, payroll, reconciliation, and AI requirements.

title: Timestamp Required on Every Audit Event
description: Each audit event shall record the ISO 8601 date and time of the action, including time zone offset. Timestamps shall be server-generated and not editable.

title: Actor Identity Required on Every Audit Event
description: Each audit event shall identify the user, system process, or AI agent that performed the action. Actor identity includes user ID, display name, and actor type (USER, SYSTEM, AI_AGENT).

title: Actor Role Recorded on Audit Event
description: Each audit event shall record the actor's role or service identity at the time of the action.

title: Affected Entity and Version Recorded
description: Each audit event shall identify the affected entity type (PLAN, ELIGIBILITY_RULE, ENROLLMENT, etc.), entity ID, and version ID where applicable.

title: Previous and New Values Recorded for Updates
description: For update operations, the system shall record the previous value and new value of each changed field. Multiple field changes in a single action shall be captured in a single audit event as an array of field-level changes.

title: Reason Code or Change Request Reference on Material Changes
description: The system shall record a reason code or change request identifier associated with material configuration changes when provided. Reason codes shall be configurable per entity type.

title: Plan Version Lifecycle Audit
description: The system shall maintain audit records for all plan version lifecycle events: creation, modification, submission for review, approval, scheduling, publication, supersession, and retirement.

title: Cross-Domain Audit Coverage
description: The system shall maintain audit records for enrollment, eligibility, carrier transactions, payroll transactions, reconciliation exceptions, open enrollment changes, continuation coverage events, ACA record changes, and life event processing.

title: AI-Generated Requirement Audit
description: The system shall maintain audit records for AI-generated requirements, including the source document, extraction context, AI agent identity, generation timestamp, and subsequent human review actions (reviewed by, review status, review timestamp).

title: Original AI-Generated Requirement Preserved After Human Edit
description: When a human edits an AI-generated requirement, the system shall preserve the original AI-generated text as a prior version. The audit trail shall clearly show: AI Generated → Human Modified → Human Approved, with each version's content accessible.

title: Audit History Search and Filtering
description: The system shall support audit history search and filtering by: user, entity type, entity ID, action type, date range, plan ID, plan version, employer group, reason, and correlation ID.

title: Audit Records Append-Only
description: Audit records shall be append-only and shall not be modifiable or deletable by standard application users. If a correction to an audit record is needed, a new corrective audit event shall be appended. The original event is preserved.

title: Correlation ID Links Related Transactions
description: The system shall associate related business operations using a common correlation ID. A single change request (e.g., plan deductible increase) shall propagate its correlation ID through audit events, enrollment impact assessments, payroll updates, carrier transactions, and reconciliation records.
demoTraceabilityChain: Change Request CR-2027-001 → Plan Version MED-PPO-500-V2 → Audit AUD-2027-000125 → Enrollment Impact IMP-2027-004 → Payroll Update PAY-2027-008 → Carrier Transaction TXN-2027-009 → Reconciliation REC-2027-002

title: Full Forward and Backward Traceability from Any Audit Event
description: The system shall allow authorized users to trace any business change forward (what downstream effects were triggered) and backward (what originating request or event caused it). For the demo: from a payroll deduction change, the user should be able to trace back to the plan configuration change and forward to the carrier transaction and reconciliation result.
demoQuestion: Show me everything that happened when the PPO-500 deductible changed from $500 to $750.
demoAnswer: User Change → Audit Event → Plan Version → Approval → AI Impact Analysis → Affected Requirements → Enrollment Impact → Payroll Update → Carrier Transaction → Reconciliation
